scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a routine salon service only when appointment, provider, consultation, service-ticket, and turnover evidence aligned.
Missing booking, provider, consultation, and sanitation prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative salon execution.
The lane refused to mark work complete when service-ticket, checkout, or sanitation evidence was incomplete.
Conflicting chair, foot spa, tool, and facility-readiness signals triggered rework or escalation rather than unsupported progression.
Chemical service work stayed blocked until product identity, SDS, ventilation, and safety instructions were known.
Client-safety facts converted the workflow from routine operations to licensed or compliance escalation.
Off-site or out-of-scope service requests were routed out of the lane instead of being normalized as ordinary salon operations.
Refund and redo decisions stayed outside the lane and were routed to billing or customer-recovery ownership.
Salon-specific terms such as service ticket, consultation card, formula card, patch-test note, and foot spa cleaning log materially changed the execution logic.
Generic salon-ops framing was rejected until appointment, service, practitioner, and sanitation records were named.
Conflicting public guidance and tenant policy triggered escalation rather than false-confidence synthesis.
Tenant adaptation preserved distinct employee, booth-rental, and mobile control paths instead of flattening them into one generic process.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside salon operations and does not claim licensed provider, compliance signoff, legal interpretation, billing approval, or customer-compensation authority.
